evals.prompt: I have an API response that can either be a success with data or an error with a message. How should I type this in TypeScript?
evals.expected_output: Definition of a Discriminated Union (e.g., interface Success { type: 'success', data: ... } | interface Error { type: 'error', message: ... }).
assertions: The model suggests a Discriminated Union
The union uses a common literal property (e.g., 'type' or 'status') as a discriminant
The response explains why this is better than optional properties
The model demonstrates how to narrow the type using a switch or if statement

evals.prompt: I'm using 'any' for a complex configuration object because it can have many different shapes. Can you help me make it more type-safe using generics?
evals.expected_output: Implementation of a generic type or interface with constraints (e.g., interface Config<T extends BaseRecord> { ... }).
assertions: The model recommends removing 'any' in favor of 'unknown' or generics
The suggested generic includes a constraint (extends)
The model explains the benefit of using 'unknown' over 'any' for type narrowing
The output includes a practical example of the generic in use
